Rice Leaf Roller.fun_fact: The larva rolls rice leaves into a tube and feeds inside, making it hard to detect early.

impact.loss_desc: Can reduce rice yield by 20-30% in severe infestations by destroying leaf area.
impact.heatmap_desc: AI indicates active leaf rolling patterns.
actions: Apply neem oil spray every 5 days

Rice Leaf Caterpillar.fun_fact: They primarily feed on the edges of rice leaves, leaving characteristic jagged bite marks.

impact.loss_desc: Defoliation reduces photosynthetic capacity, leading to lighter grain weight.

hazard.first_aid: Wash skin if touched to avoid mild allergic rash.
hazard.precautions: Wear long sleeves when walking through infested fields.

Paddy Stem Maggot.fun_fact: The adult is a tiny gray fly that lays eggs on rice leaves; the maggots mine into the stems.

impact.loss_desc: Causes 'deadheart' (drying of the central shoot), leading to 10-40% yield loss.
actions: Drain water from field for 3-4 days

hazard.precautions: Flies can carry minor agricultural pathogens.

Asiatic Rice Borer.fun_fact: One of the most destructive pests of rice globally, it overwinters in the stubble left after harvest.

impact.loss_desc: Complete destruction of tillers leading to 'whiteheads' and massive yield loss.
impact.heatmap_desc: AI detects entry holes in stems.

hazard.precautions: Handle chemical treatments with severe care.

Brown Plant Hopper.fun_fact: They suck sap directly from the plant's vascular system, causing the plant to dry out entirely, known as 'hopperburn'.

impact.loss_desc: Can wipe out entire fields overnight if 'hopperburn' sets in.
impact.heatmap_desc: AI detects swarming near the plant base.
actions: Spray contact insecticides at the plant base

hazard.first_aid: Wash hands. Can cause severe respiratory allergies if inhaled in swarms.
hazard.precautions: Wear a mask when walking through heavily infested 'hopperburn' areas.

Aphids.fun_fact: Aphids can reproduce asexually, allowing their populations to explode in just a few days.

impact.loss_desc: Stunts plant growth and transmits debilitating viral diseases.
impact.heatmap_desc: AI focuses on dense clusters on undersides of leaves.

hazard.precautions: Harmless to humans, but honeydew excretion can attract stinging ants.

Army Worm.fun_fact: Named because they literally march across fields in massive lines, consuming everything in their path.

impact.loss_desc: Can devour entire fields of seedlings overnight.

hazard.precautions: Caterpillar hairs may cause mild skin irritation.

Default.fun_fact: This insect has been identified by the custom AI model. Refer to general pest control guidelines.

impact.loss_desc: Impact depends on specific local conditions and population density.
impact.heatmap_desc: AI focus map generated over the insect body.
actions: Consult local agricultural extension for tailored advice

hazard.first_aid: Exercise standard caution. Wash hands after handling.
hazard.precautions: Avoid direct contact with unknown species.
